_comment: EP3-T5 passage-fidelity audit findings, machine-readable. Authored from
THIS IS A SYNTHETIC ADVERSARIAL REVIEW (gpt-5.6-terra, cross-family lens). It is NOT clinical
validation, NOT credentialed clinical review, and confers no release authority. It is used here
only to DE-CLAIM: every flag below can suppress or withhold a claim, never add or strengthen one.
Both scripts/evidence/vendor-rf-bundle.mjs and scripts/evidence/build-evidence-pack.mjs read this
file, so every deviation of the shipped KB from the raw RF-EV-001 bundle is traceable to a numbered
finding. Findings are applied mechanically; no clinical prose is authored by this pipeline.
Note on causation: most of these defects originate in the RF-EV-001 bundle's own `summary` fields,
which the converter copied faithfully. They are content defects in the upstream evidence bundle,
not converter bugs -- which is precisely why an independent cross-family audit was in the plan.

flagSemantics.source-not-independently-retrievable: The cited source artifact could not be independently retrieved or rights-cleared, so a located-passage claim against it cannot be substantiated from outside the RF bundle's own self-attestation. Blocks source-supported binding.
flagSemantics.overclaims-source-modality: The passage states as mandatory ('must', 'is confirmed') what the located source states conditionally ('might need to be', 'should'). Blocks source-supported binding.
flagSemantics.adds-claim-not-in-located-passage: The passage asserts something the located passage does not support. Blocks source-supported binding.
flagSemantics.omits-source-numerics: The passage drops thresholds, units, age bands, or formulae that the located passage carries. The passage is incomplete rather than false; it must not be used as executable grounding. Blocks source-supported binding.
flagSemantics.near-verbatim-span-pending-rights: The passage text shares a contiguous span with restricted source text long enough to constitute reproduction while REG-002 is uncleared. Triggers WITHHOLDING of the passage text, not merely a flag.

bindingRule: A passage carrying ANY flag MUST NOT be bound as source-supported grounding for a rule. EP-4 falls back to that source's implementation-proposal sentinel. This is fail-safe by construction: the direction of error is always toward 'we do not claim this is source-backed.'

findings.claim: Passage text reproduces 8-13 contiguous words of restricted source text despite passageFidelity: paraphrase. The span originates in the RF bundle's own summary field.

The AAP source card provides only a publisher URL with file_path: null plus a self-attestation of full-text access, not a retrievable artifact. RF-EV-002 and REG-002 independently recorded HTTP 403 / subscription-required for this source. Shipping these as source-supported in a clinical KB is an over-claim.
Passage asserts the AAP Table 1 intervals are 'used as the KB fallback' — an implementation claim the located passage does not make — while dropping the Hb/MCV/RDW numeric intervals it does carry.
Passage states only that four pediatric haemoglobin bands exist, dropping every cutoff (<105, <110, <115, <120 g/L) and its age/sex band.
Passage refers to 'a stated increment table and quadratic equation' without the equation, its coefficients, units, or adjustment direction.
Passage drops the 30C/37C adult G6PD reference values and the 6-PGD-correction distinction the located passage carries.

Passage adds that transfusion is reserved for haemodynamic instability; the located passage supports only Hb <7 g/dL and possible minimal symptoms. Adding an unsourced transfusion statement to a pediatric CDS knowledge base is the most dangerous single defect in this audit.
Located passage says the factors 'might need to be considered'; the passage says they 'must be considered'.
Passage adds that CRP is unnecessary in a clinically well child; the located passage supports only that ferritin is unreliable when CRP is elevated in illness/inflammation.
Located passage says providers 'should collect a venous sample'; the passage says it 'must be confirmed before further action', adding a sequencing claim.

nonPassageFindings.claim: build-evidence-pack.mjs orders records with localeCompare(), which is locale-dependent, so byte determinism is not established across environments.
nonPassageFindings.remediation: Replace with an explicit ASCII/codepoint comparator.
